text.title: Facilitation rules, problem fixes, and convergence methods
text.summary: Facilitator rules for before, during, and after a session; specific interventions for common failure modes (dominant talker, energy dip, off-topic drift); dot voting and impact/effort scoring for convergence.

rule.statement: Define a specific, single-question objective before the session. "Discuss the product" is not an objective. "Generate 20+ ideas for reducing onboarding churn from 40% to below 25%" is.
rule.rationale: A vague objective produces vague output. A specific question with a quantity target gives the group a clear success criterion.

rule.statement: Invite 4-8 people with diverse backgrounds and roles. More than 10 makes facilitation unmanageable. Fewer than 4 lacks the cross-pollination that produces non-obvious ideas.
rule.rationale: Diversity of perspective is the primary predictor of idea novelty in brainstorming research.

section.title: During session — common problems and fixes

list.item: Dominant talker: switch to Brainwriting or Round Robin to enforce structured turns
Criticism creeping in: "Hold that thought for the evaluation phase"
Running out of ideas: apply a constraint ("what if budget were 10x?"), change perspective ("what would a user say?"), use SCAMPER
Off-topic drift: "Parking lot" — capture the idea on a separate board for later
Group too quiet: individual silent writing for 2 min before sharing

rule.statement: Give each participant 3-5 dots. Participants vote on their favorite ideas from the clustered list. Top-voted ideas move forward for evaluation. No discussion during voting.
rule.rationale: Dot voting externalizes preferences simultaneously, preventing the anchoring effect of verbal polling where the first opinion heard shapes all subsequent votes.

rule.statement: Score each idea on Impact (weight 3x) and Feasibility (weight 2x). Avoid over-weighting cost and time (1x each) — cheap but low-impact ideas should not win over expensive but transformative ones.
rule.rationale: Default human judgment over-weights effort and under-weights impact. Explicit weighting corrects this bias.

list.item: Quick Wins: high impact, low effort — implement first
Major Projects: high impact, high effort — schedule and resource
Fill-ins: low impact, low effort — optional backlog
Time Sinks: low impact, high effort — reject

antipattern.description: Skipping the convergence step after ideation — leaving participants with a list but no prioritization or owner assignments.
antipattern.reason: Ideas without owners and priorities evaporate. The session feels productive but produces no action, which destroys trust in future brainstorming sessions.

text.reference: Session plan template with agenda and results capture
Prompt for bulk idea generation pass
Prompt for clustering idea list into themes
